author: Ňõrńęla saccidńĀnanda bhaktibinoda ŠĻ≠hńĀkura
verses:
hari he!
jagatera vastu jata, baddha saba svabhńĀvataŠł•,
deŇõa‚čÖkńĀla‚čÖvastu‚čÖsńęmńĀŇõraye
tumi prabhu sarveŇõvara, naha sńęmńĀ‚čÖvidhipara,

sama vńĀ adhika tava, svabhńĀvataŠł• asambhava,
vidhi laŠĻÖghi‚Äô tava avasthńĀna
svatantra‚čÖsvabhńĀva dhara, ńĀpane gopana kara,

tathńĀpi ananya‚čÖbhakta, tomńĀre dekhite Ňõakta,
sadńĀ dekhe svarŇępa tomńĀra
e bhaktivinoda dńęna, ananyabhajana‚čÖhńęna,

en_translation:
Hari! All things in the world are inherently bound, limited by space, time, and material objects. You, Lord, are the master of all, not subject to any limits or rules; all regulations tremble in fear of You.

An equal to or greater than You is naturally impossible; Your existence transcends all rules. You possess an independent nature, You conceal Yourself, manifesting through the power of MńĀyńĀ.

Yet, exclusive devotees are capable of seeing You, always perceiving Your true form. This Bhaktivinoda, humble and devoid of exclusive worship, considers only the dust of the devotees' feet as his true essence.
